final_answer:
No, it is still partial; there are 4 concrete parts left.

Why it matters: Gold Chain validator admission is still in a mixed transition, so you can still get architecture drift from the final production/BSC-style target.

Confirmed read-only gaps:
1. Bridge still queues validator-join tasks from root-chain `Staked` logs in [rootchain_log.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/bridge/listener/rootchain_log.go:82).
2. The join task handler still runs old prechecks/retry logic before the final skip, so the disabled path still consumes bridge/runtime flow in [stake.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/bridge/processor/stake.go:111).
3. `MsgValidatorJoin` still carries legacy bridge fields (`tx_hash`, `log_index`, `block_number`, `nonce`) in [tx.proto](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/proto/giltconsensusv2/stake/tx.proto:51).
4. Validator-set selection is still epoch/activity based, not stake-ranked/capped BSC-style election logic in [validator.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/x/stake/types/validator.go:53), [validator_set.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/x/stake/types/validator_set.go:617), and [validator.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/x/stake/keeper/validator.go:564).

Implementation plan (max 5 steps):
1. Remove bridge `Staked` → `sendValidatorJoinToGiltConsensus` enqueue/registration path fully.
2. Remove join legacy fields from proto/message structs and regenerate `tx.pb.go` cleanly.
3. Replace current validator-set update logic with stake-ranked, capped active-set election semantics.
4. Add focused tests for: no bridge join ingestion, join sender/signer auth, and ranked-election behavior.
5. Run full stake + bridge checks and only mark complete if all pass.

Read-only verification run: `go test ./x/stake/...`, `go test ./bridge/processor -run TestStakingProcessor -count=1`, `go test ./app -run '^$'` all passed. No code was changed in this audit.
prompt:
